objective: Implement deterministic compiler conservation primitives from P1-SPEC-07 without adding product semantics.
implemented_capabilities: Deterministic final DRD assembly from a closed approved input bundle.
Stable FINAL_DRD.md rendering with fixed title, TOC, separators, source attribution, and preserved approved section body text.
Structured final manifest, TOC, reference index, hash index, conservation report, and compiled semantic inventory outputs.
Semantic, mechanical, input bundle, TOC, reference, conservation, and full output hash partitions.
Atomic semantic unit validation for required fields, approved unit types, unit hashes, narrow source spans, and parent-child proof boundaries.
Semantic conservation comparison for matched, added, omitted, and hash-drifted units.
Compiler input bundle validation for closed fields, allowed input types, structured approvals, invalidated evidence rejection, and stage order.
Hash drift, deterministic section ordering, final manifest, conservation report, and DRD-06 read-only QA boundary validators.
Compiler schemas and positive/negative tests for conservative assembly and validation failures.

Review patch adds fail-closed compilation, approved-versus-compiled inventory comparison, closed input hash verification, full manifest hash and count checks, stricter input record schema, and structural parent-child inventory checks.

deferred_due_scope.item: Repository-wide extraction of real DRD-01 through DRD-04 artifacts
deferred_due_scope.reason: This workpack implements deterministic compiler primitives and validators; full artifact discovery belongs to an explicit compiler orchestration workpack.

deferred_due_scope.item: Writing final DRD files into a canonical release directory
deferred_due_scope.reason: This candidate returns structured compiled outputs and does not create final release authority or BUILD_LOCK.

deferred_due_scope.item: Human Gate final approval and lock creation
deferred_due_scope.reason: The compiler cannot approve, promote, or lock its own output.
